Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types:\\n\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`\\n- `AdvancedThreatProtection`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library\\n- **`Examples` section is empty** — no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc.\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow\\n- **No sample files exist** — `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`**\\n\\nReplace the empty sections with content as follows:\\n\\n1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed — the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI.\\n\\n2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule.\\n\\n3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to:\\n   - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n   - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started\\n   - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation\\n\\nExample README diff for the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServer server = new(\\u0026#34;mysqlServer\\u0026#34;, MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n{\\n    Location = AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n    Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n    {\\n        Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D4ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n        Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose,\\n    },\\n};\\ninfra.Add(server);\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\nMySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new(\\u0026#34;allowAll\\u0026#34;)\\n{\\n    StartIpAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n    EndIpAddress = \\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;,\\n    Parent = server,\\n};\\ninfra.Add(firewallRule);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs`\\n\\n   Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should:\\n   - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`\\n   - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method\\n   - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\n   Suggested snippets:\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` — demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n\\n   Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape.\\n\\n2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format:\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. The package adds declarative provisioning support for Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Servers, including these resource types:\\n\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerConfiguration`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerAadAdministrator`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerBackup` / `MySqlFlexibleServerBackupV2`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServerMaintenance`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersCapability`\\n- `MySqlFlexibleServersPrivateEndpointConnection`\\n- `AdvancedThreatProtection`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`Authenticate the Client` section is empty** — no guidance on how to authenticate when using the provisioning library\\n- **`Examples` section is empty** — no code snippets showing how to create a MySQL Flexible Server, add a firewall rule, configure a database, etc.\\n- **`Next steps` section is empty** — no links to related docs, samples, or the Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) workflow\\n- **No sample files exist** — `tests/Samples/` directory does not exist; there are no test-backed snippets for snippet extraction\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**Edit `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md`**\\n\\nReplace the empty sections with content as follows:\\n\\n1. Fill `### Authenticate the Client` with a note that no explicit authentication is needed — the provisioning library generates Bicep/ARM templates; authentication occurs when deploying via `azd` or the Azure CLI.\\n\\n2. Fill `## Examples` with at least two snippet placeholders: one for creating a basic MySQL Flexible Server and one for adding a firewall rule.\\n\\n3. Fill `## Next steps` with links to:\\n   - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md)\\n   - Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) getting started\\n   - Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server documentation\\n\\nExample README diff for the `## Examples` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a MySQL Flexible Server\\n\\n```\\nInfrastructure infra = new();\\n\\nMySqlFlexibleServer server = new(\\u0026#34;mysqlServer\\u0026#34;, MySqlFlexibleServer.ResourceVersions.V2024_12_30)\\n{\\n    Location = AzureLocation.EastUS,\\n    Sku = new MySqlFlexibleServerSku\\n    {\\n        Name = \\u0026#34;Standard_D4ds_v4\\u0026#34;,\\n        Tier = ServerSkuTier.GeneralPurpose,\\n    },\\n};\\ninfra.Add(server);\\n```\\n\\n### Add a Firewall Rule\\n\\n```\\nMySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule firewallRule = new(\\u0026#34;allowAll\\u0026#34;)\\n{\\n    StartIpAddress = \\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;,\\n    EndIpAddress = \\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;,\\n    Parent = server,\\n};\\ninfra.Add(firewallRule);\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Create the sample test file** at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/tests/Samples/Sample1_MySqlProvisioning.cs`\\n\\n   Use `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets` samples as the canonical example for test file structure. The file should:\\n   - Wrap examples in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`\\n   - Use `[Test]` (NUnit) attribute on each method\\n   - Wrap test-only teardown/assertions in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\n   Suggested snippets:\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer` — demonstrates creating a `MySqlFlexibleServer`\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddFirewallRule` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerFirewallRule` as a child resource\\n   - `MySqlProvisioningAddDatabase` — demonstrates adding a `MySqlFlexibleServerDatabase`\\n\\n   Reference an existing provisioning sample, e.g. `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/tests/Samples/`, for the test class shape.\\n\\n2. **Add snippet placeholders in `README.md`** under `## Examples` in the exact format:\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:MySqlProvisioningCreateFlexibleServer\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/mysql/Azure.Provisioning.MySql/README.md` must contain these sections. The issue is a documentation gap, not a code defect.\n\n**Suggested investigation steps:**\n1.
